question.text: Angel funds shall invest in startups that are not related to an industrial group whose combined turnover exceeds:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Angel funds shall invest in startups which are not promoted or sponsored by or related to an industrial group whose group turnover (combined) exceeds Rs 300 crores. **The Trap:** This restriction ensures angel funds focus on genuine early-stage ventures, not subsidiaries of large groups. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This prevents established business houses from accessing angel fund capital through shell startups.
